proposed_hypotheses.text: PFS in months is non-uniformly distributed across the 50,000-patient cohort, with a clinically plausible mean (3-7 months) and a small fraction of patients with pfs_months at the extremes (0 or near the maximum), motivating use of pfs_months as a continuous outcome with linear models.

analyses.result_summary: pfs_months mean=4.69 (SD 2.50), median=4.54, range 0.00-16.66; 1219 patients (2.4%) have pfs_months=0 and 1 patient at the maximum. Distribution is roughly symmetric and supports OLS as a first-pass model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 18 binary features (each coded 0/1), at least one feature has a clinically meaningful univariate association with mean pfs_months in either direction; specifically feature_006=1 and feature_001=1 are associated with shorter PFS while feature_013=1 is associated with longer PFS.

analyses.code: for c in binary_cols: stats.ttest_ind(df.loc[df[c]==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df[c]==0,'pfs_months'], equal_var=False)
analyses.result_summary: Largest signed mean PFS differences (=1 minus =0): feature_006 -1.54 mo (p~0), feature_013 +1.10 mo (p~0), feature_001 -1.05 mo (p=1.5e-178), feature_028 -0.56 mo (p=6e-144), feature_029 +0.57 mo (p=4e-143), feature_018 -0.46 mo (p=6e-70), feature_023 +0.36 mo (p=6e-59), feature_016 +0.11 mo (p=1e-6). feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_009, feature_026, feature_032, feature_008 had |diff|<0.04 with p>=0.05 (no main effect).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the continuous features, feature_012 (range 30-90, mean 65, plausibly age) is the strongest continuous correlate of pfs_months, with higher feature_012 associated with longer PFS.
feature_005 is an ordinal performance-status-like variable (values 0,1,2) with higher values associated with shorter PFS in a monotonic dose-response.

analyses.code: for c in cont_cols: stats.spearmanr(df[c], df['pfs_months']); df.groupby('feature_005')['pfs_months'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: feature_012 had a very strong positive Spearman rho=+0.726 (p~0) with pfs_months; mean PFS rose monotonically across feature_012 deciles from 0.6 mo (age 30-40) to 8.2 mo (age 80+). feature_005 showed the expected monotonic pattern: PS=0 mean 5.64 mo, PS=1 mean 4.44 mo, PS=2 mean 3.29 mo. Other notable correlations: feature_004 rho=-0.116, feature_024 rho=+0.102, feature_033 rho=-0.083 (all p<1e-77).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable OLS model with all 37 features as predictors of pfs_months, the strongest independent predictors will be feature_012 (positive), feature_005 (negative), feature_006 (negative), feature_013 (positive), feature_001 (negative), feature_028 (negative), feature_024 (positive), feature_004 (negative), feature_029 (positive) and feature_018 (negative); features that were null univariately (feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, etc.) will remain null.

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.792 (adj 0.792). Top |t| predictors: feature_012 beta=+0.176/unit (t=343), feature_005 beta=-1.17/level (t=-156), feature_006 beta=-1.55 (t=-139), feature_013 beta=+1.10 (t=+103), feature_001 beta=-0.98 (t=-58), feature_004 beta=-0.077/unit (t=-58), feature_028 beta=-0.58 (t=-54), feature_024 beta=+0.48/unit (t=+47), feature_033 beta=-0.019/unit (t=-45), feature_029 beta=+0.53 (t=+36), feature_018 beta=-0.47 (t=-33). feature_011 (p=0.21), feature_030 (p=0.08), feature_019 (p=0.42), feature_023 (p=0.32), feature_009 (p=0.04), feature_032 (p=0.05), feature_008 (p=0.12) all consistent with no independent main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 (35% prevalence, +1.10 month adjusted main effect, balanced indicator-style coding) is a candidate treatment variable. feature_023 (also positive, balanced) was non-significant after adjustment, suggesting it is a co-marker rather than an independent treatment.

feature_011 and feature_030, with prevalences near 50% and adjusted p>0.05, are most consistent with randomized but ineffective indicators (placebo treatment arms or chance allocations); they should not show meaningful effect heterogeneity.

analyses.code: From multivariable OLS coefficients in iteration 4.
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 selected as primary treatment candidate based on adjusted +1.10 mo benefit (p~0). feature_011 and feature_030 selected as null-treatment controls.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit from feature_013 is heterogeneous across binary subgroups: feature_029=1 and feature_023=1 amplify the benefit while feature_018=1 and feature_028=1 abolish it; the corresponding interaction terms in covariate-adjusted OLS will be highly significant.

analyses.result_summary: All four predicted interactions are highly significant (each p<1e-50). feature_013 x feature_029 interaction beta=+1.56 (p~0); main feature_013 collapses to +0.012 when feature_029=0. feature_013 x feature_023 interaction beta=+1.03 (p~0); main feature_013 +0.44 when feature_023=0. feature_013 x feature_018 interaction beta=-1.34 (p~0); feature_013 x feature_028 interaction beta=-1.68 (p~0). Other modifiers had |beta|<0.4. Pattern: f029=1 and f023=1 are 'enabler' biomarkers; f018=1 and f028=1 are 'suppressor' biomarkers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous features, feature_033 (range 1-100, plausibly a tumor burden / disease activity marker) is the strongest continuous modifier of feature_013's PFS benefit: the benefit is concentrated in patients with low feature_033 and disappears at high feature_033.

analyses.result_summary: feature_013 x feature_033 interaction beta=-0.0532/unit (p~0): each one-unit rise in feature_033 reduces feature_013's PFS benefit by 0.053 months. No other continuous feature came close (next-best feature_010 inter -0.054, p=0.012). Stratifying by feature_033 quintile within the cohort: benefit was +1.80 (q1, f33=1-6.7), +1.95 (q2), +1.71 (q3), +0.03 (q4, f33=14.7-22), -0.01 (q5, f33>=22). The transition is sharp around feature_033 = 14-15.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists a sharp threshold around feature_033 = 14 such that patients with feature_033<=14 retain the full feature_013 PFS benefit (~+5 mo) and patients with feature_033>14 have essentially no benefit (~0 mo).

analyses.code: for t in thresholds: stratify base subgroup (f29=1 & f18=0 & f28=0) by f033<=t
analyses.result_summary: Threshold scan within base subgroup (f029=1 & f018=0 & f028=0): f033<=10 diff=+4.96, f033<=12 diff=+4.96, f033<=14 diff=+4.94, f033<=16 diff=+4.42, f033<=20 diff=+3.83, f033<=25 diff=+3.39. In the complementary tails: f033>14 diff=+0.02, f033>16 diff=-0.02, f033>20 diff=-0.03. Decile analysis within base subgroup confirmed the cliff: deciles 1-5 (f033 1-12.2) all gave diff between +4.87 and +5.03; decile 6 (f033 12.3-14.6) +3.97; decile 7 (f033 14.7-17.6) +0.17; deciles 8-10 within +/-0.23. Optimal binary cutoff is feature_033<=14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Defining responder subgroup S = {feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14}, the feature_013 effect is approximately +5 months PFS inside S and approximately 0 outside S; the formal feature_013 x S interaction effect estimate exceeds +4 months and is highly significant in a covariate-adjusted model.

analyses.result_summary: Subgroup S contains 10,828 patients (21.7% of cohort). Inside S: feature_013=1 mean PFS 9.23, feature_013=0 mean PFS 4.29, raw diff +4.94. Outside S: feature_013=1 mean 4.32, feature_013=0 mean 4.32, raw diff +0.007. Adjusted global model with feature_013 x S interaction: feature_013 main beta -0.0001 (p=0.99), S main beta -0.011 (p=0.04), interaction beta +4.68 (p~0). Model R^2 jumps from 0.79 (no interaction) to 0.976 with the interaction term. Effect within S is homogeneous across PS levels: PS=0 diff +4.97, PS=1 +4.93, PS=2 +5.00.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each predicate of S is necessary: dropping feature_029=1, feature_018=0, feature_028=0, or feature_033<=14 from the definition reduces the in-subgroup feature_013 effect; dropping feature_023=1 (a candidate fifth predicate) does not improve the effect because feature_023 is redundant with feature_029 (Pearson r=+0.67).

analyses.code: Run feature_013 effect estimation in each ablated subgroup; corr(f029,f023)
analyses.result_summary: Ablations (vs reference S2 = S + feature_023=1, in-subgroup diff +4.71 with covariate-adjusted beta +4.69): drop f029 -> +4.39 (smaller, f029 critical), drop f023 -> +4.68 (no change, redundant), drop f018=0 -> +3.89, drop f028=0 -> +3.07, drop f033<=14 -> +2.95. Pearson correlation feature_029 vs feature_023 = +0.669, confirming redundancy of feature_023 given feature_029. Final parsimonious subgroup retained: f029=1 & f018=0 & f028=0 & f033<=14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_011 and feature_030, the two binary indicators with adjusted p>0.05 and prevalence near 50%, will fail to show meaningful treatment-effect heterogeneity with any other binary feature, supporting their interpretation as null/randomized indicators rather than active treatments.
feature_006 (strong negative main effect, -1.55 mo) is a prognostic marker rather than a treatment: it will not show large heterogeneity-of-effect interactions with biomarkers like feature_029.

analyses.code: Heterogeneity scan for feature_011, feature_030, feature_019, feature_006
analyses.result_summary: feature_011: largest interaction was feature_011 x feature_013 inter +0.046 (p=0.03), trivial in magnitude. feature_030: largest interaction inter +0.036 (p=0.09). feature_019: largest interaction with feature_023 inter -0.069 (p=1e-3). feature_006: largest interaction (feature_006 x feature_015) -0.046 (p=0.06), no biomarker amplifying or suppressing effect. None of these came close to feature_013's interaction magnitudes (>1 month). This supports h13 and h14: feature_011/feature_030 act as null indicators, feature_006 acts as a uniformly prognostic factor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The single best-supported treatment-by-subgroup hypothesis for the pfs_months outcome is: 'In patients with feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14, treatment with feature_013 increases pfs_months by approximately +4.7 to +4.9 months relative to no feature_013; in patients failing any of these four predicates, feature_013 has no effect on pfs_months.' No other binary feature in the dataset is a plausible treatment with a similarly heterogeneous effect.

analyses.code: Final confirmation: stratified means + covariate-adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: Final responder subgroup S = (feature_029=1 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_033<=14): n=10828 (21.7%). Inside S: mean PFS 9.23 mo on feature_013 vs 4.29 mo off feature_013 (raw diff +4.94, p~0). Outside S: 4.32 vs 4.32 (raw diff +0.007, p=0.88). Covariate-adjusted feature_013 x S interaction beta = +4.68 (p~0) with main feature_013 beta = 0.0 (p=0.99). The biomarker constraints can be interpreted as: feature_029=1 (treatment target present), feature_033<=14 (low burden / favorable disease state), feature_018=0 and feature_028=0 (no resistance markers). feature_005 (PS) does not modulate the in-S benefit (effect identical across PS=0,1,2).
